summary: Tested Billing Migration Studio backend (15 pytest tests covering auth, sources, schema, validate, mappings, migrations dry/actual/retry/rollback/records/logs/report, dashboard, customers) and frontend (login + dashboard render with real stats). All 15 backend tests pass after fixing a Stripe import bug. Frontend login flow works; dashboard correctly shows backend data (4 runs, 3 sources, 3 customers migrated) confirming end-to-end integration with httpOnly cookies.

minor.issue: FIXED by testing agent: infer_schema crashed with AttributeError 'float' object has no attribute strip when stripe mock returned numeric values. Patched services/csv_service.py to coerce to str before strip().

action_items: Backend CORS is configured with allow_origins=['*'] and allow_credentials=False. Frontend uses axios withCredentials=true. This works only because the frontend and backend are served from the same external origin via the Kubernetes ingress. If the app is ever served from a different origin, browsers will refuse to send cookies. Consider setting allow_credentials=True with explicit origins for production.
Cookies are set with secure=False; for production deployments behind HTTPS, set secure=True.

auth_testing.md prerequisites: bcrypt hashes verified to start with $2b (bcrypt.gensalt default). seed_admin updates password if changed - verified. No brute-force lockout is implemented (NOT required by problem statement, but flagged per playbook test instructions).
critical_code_review_comments: services/csv_service.py infer_schema assumed string values; fixed to coerce row.get(h) to str before strip().
transformation.py uses dotted canonical paths (e.g., customer.email); frontend NewMigration must use these canonical paths in mappings - auto_suggest_mapping returns correct dotted paths.

migration_runner.py wraps individual record writes in try/except but does not catch DB integrity errors on customer email uniqueness; consider db.rollback() per failed record to avoid breaking subsequent inserts.
Rollback marks records 'rolled_back' but never resets MigrationRun.successful_records, which can leave stale counts on the dashboard for rolled-back runs.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login + dashboard verified, sidebar navigation present with Data Sources/Mappings/Migrations/Customers, stats populated correctly from API)

seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_dry, TEST_actual migration runs and 3 customers via CSV mapping; one actual run was rolled back at end of test. CSV source and stripe mock source created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_billing_studio.py is the regression suite (15 tests, all green). It uses requests.Session with cookie auth. Re-run via: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_billing_studio.py -v. The csv_service.py float strip fix is in place; if reverted, stripe import will 500.
rca_of_the_issue: Stripe import 500: services/stripe_service mock returns rows with float fields (e.g., monthly_fee=29.0). csv_service.infer_schema called (row.get(h) or '').strip() which fails on float because float has no .strip(). Reproduction: POST /api/sources/stripe?limit=20 with empty STRIPE_SECRET_KEY. Mitigation applied: coerce value via str(val) before strip().
